heading: BIBLIOGRAFIA 2819
card_summary: This chunk is a section of the scholarly bibliography for Saverio Campanini's 2010 Bompiani edition of Francesco Zorzi's De Harmonia Mundi, covering entries from CAMPANINI 2000 through DAN 1972. The entries document Campanini's own extensive publications on Christian Kabbalah, Flavio Mitridate's Latin translations of kabbalistic texts for Pico della Mirandola, the Sefer Bahir, the Sefer Yetzirah, and Pietro Galatino's defense of Christian Kabbalah. Other cited scholars include Gershom Scholem, Delio Cantimori on Nicodemism, André Chastel on Marsilio Ficino and art, and Umberto Cassuto on Hebrew manuscripts in the Vatican. The bibliography spans Italian, German, French, and English publications across journals including Materia Giudaica, European Journal of Jewish Studies, and Jerusalem Studies in Jewish Thought, reflecting the multilingual international scholarship on Renaissance Jewish-Christian intellectual exchange.
essay:
# Bibliography Section: CAMPANINI 2000 – DAN 1972

## Nature and Organization

This chunk constitutes a portion of the comprehensive scholarly bibliography appended to Saverio Campanini's critical Italian edition of Francesco Zorzi's *De Harmonia Mundi* (Bompiani, 2010). The bibliography follows a standard author-date citation format used throughout the apparatus of the edition. Entries are sorted alphabetically by author surname and then chronologically within an author's works. Where a single author produced multiple publications in the same year, disambiguating lowercase letters are appended to the date (e.g., CAMPANINI 2002a, 2002b).

The section running from pp. 2983–2989 is notably dominated by the editor's own scholarly output. Saverio Campanini — who also prepared the critical edition — is himself one of the foremost authorities on Christian Kabbalah in the Renaissance, and his bibliography here constitutes a virtual research curriculum on the field. Entries by other scholars (Cantimori, Cassuto, Chastel, Corazzol, Dagron–Ansaldi, Dahan, Dan, and others) fill out the coverage of adjacent topics: Venetian religious history, Hebrew manuscript studies, Marsilio Ficino, Leon Ebreo's *Dialoghi d'amore*, and medieval Jewish philosophy.

## Scholarly Value

The bibliography provides several types of scholarly value. First, it maps the modern secondary literature on Christian Kabbalah from Pico della Mirandola through Francesco Zorzi and into the seventeenth century (including Jacques Gaffarel). Second, it documents the critical scholarship on Flavio Mitridate (Guglielmo Raimondo Moncada), the Sicilian Jewish convert whose Latin translations of kabbalistic texts shaped the Christian reception of Kabbalah in Renaissance Italy. Third, it identifies the key journal venues — *Materia Giudaica*, *European Journal of Jewish Studies*, *Jerusalem Studies in Jewish Thought*, *La Bibliofilia* — where this specialized research appears. Fourth, it signals the multilingual nature of the field: entries appear in Italian, German, French, English, and Spanish, reflecting the dispersed institutional locations of Renaissance Kabbalah studies.

## Sample Entries (verbatim)

**1.** CAMPANINI 2002 — S. Campanini, *Pici Mirandulensis Bibliotheca Cabbalistica Latina. Sulle traduzioni latine di opere cabbalistiche eseguite da Flavio Mitridate per Pico della Mirandola*, in «Materia Giudaica» 7/1 (2002), pp. 90-96.

**2.** CAMPANINI 2006a — S. Campanini, *Ein unbekannter Kommentar zum „Hohelied" aus der kabbalistischen Schule von Francesco Zorzi*, in G. Frank – A. Hallacker – S. Lalla (edd.), *Erzählende Vernunft*, Akademie Verlag, Berlin 2006, pp. 265-281.

**3.** CAMPANINI 2007b — S. Campanini, *Francesco Zorzi: armonia del mondo e filosofia simbolica*, in A. Angelini – P. Caye (a cura di), *Il pensiero simbolico nella prima età moderna*, Olschki, Firenze 2007, pp. 225-246.

**4.** CANTIMORI 1959 — D. Cantimori, *«Nicodemismo» e speranze conciliari nel Cinquecento*, in Id., *Studi di storia*, Einaudi, Torino 1959, pp. 528-529.

**5.** CORAZZOL 2008 — G. Corazzol (ed.), M. Recanati, *Commentary on the Daily Prayers. The Hebrew Text, Flavius Mithridates' Latin Translation, and an English Version*, Nino Aragno Editore, Torino 2008.

## Observations on Coverage

The concentration of Campanini entries (roughly twenty-five items spanning 2000–2011) within a single bibliography section is unusual and reflects the circumstances of the edition: the editor is also the principal modern scholar of Zorzi and of Christian Kabbalah more broadly, making self-citation unavoidable and editorially transparent. Several entries marked *in corso di stampa* (forthcoming at time of publication) indicate the bibliography was compiled in 2010–2011 as the edition went to press. The range of topics covered — from the *Sefer Bahir* and *Sefer Yetzirah* to Pietro Galatino, from Gershom Scholem's reception history to Venetian Franciscan thought — confirms that the apparatus treats Zorzi's *De Harmonia Mundi* as a nexus connecting Jewish mysticism, Neoplatonism, biblical philology, and Catholic reform theology.
